---
package: "openpipeline"
version: "v4.2.0"
title: "cellranger_mkgtf"
namespace: "Reference"
description: "Make a GTF file - filter by a specific attribute."
type: "module"
toc: false
latest: "true"
subtitle: "openpipeline v4.2.0"
---

::: {.column-margin}

### Info

ID: `cellranger_mkgtf`  
Namespace: `reference`

### Links

[{{< fa brands github >}} View source code](https://github.com/openpipelines-bio/openpipeline/tree/v4.2.0/src/reference/cellranger_mkgtf)

[![](/images/viash-hub-logo.svg){.vh-icon} Browse all versions](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline)

[![](/images/viash-hub-logo.svg){.vh-icon} Get run instructions](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline/v4.2.0/components/reference/cellranger_mkgtf)

:::




## Argument group

### Arguments

|Name           |Description                                                                                            |Attributes                                                                                                                    |
|:----------|:--------------------------------------------------|:--------------------|
|`--input_gtf`  |Reference GTF annotation.                                                                              |`file`, required, example: `"transcriptome_annotation.gtf.gz"`                                                                |
|`--output_gtf` |Output GTF file.                                                                                       |`file`, required, example: `"output.gtf.gz"`                                                                                  |
|`--attribute`  |Key-value pair in attributes field to be kept in the GTF file of the format attribute:attribute_value. |List of `string`, required, example: `"gene_type:transcribed_unprocessed_pseudogene", "gene_type:miRNA"`, multiple_sep: `";"` |

## Authors


  * Jakub Majercik [{{< fa brands github >}}](https://github.com/jakubmajercik) [{{< fa brands linkedin >}}](https://linkedin.com/in/jakubmajercik) (author)

## Example commands

You can run the pipeline using `nextflow run`.

### View help

You can use `--help` as a parameter to get an overview of the possible parameters.

```bash
nextflow run openpipelines-bio/openpipeline \
  -r v4.2.0 -latest \
  -main-script target/nextflow/reference/cellranger_mkgtf/main.nf \
  --help
```

### Run command

<details>
<summary>Example of `params.yaml`</summary>

Generate a `params.yaml` for this component on [Viash Hub](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline/v4.2.0/components/reference/cellranger_mkgtf).

</details>

Run locally with:

```bash
nextflow run openpipelines-bio/openpipeline \
  -r v4.2.0 -latest \
  -profile docker \
  -main-script target/nextflow/reference/cellranger_mkgtf/main.nf \
  -params-file params.yaml
```

:::{.callout-note}
Replace `-profile docker` with `-profile podman` or `-profile singularity` depending on the desired backend.
:::

Running on Seqera or through Viash Hub is explained in the [guides](/guides/index.qmd).
